page: Vultr vbm-48c-1024gb-4-a100-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dedicated compute resources and high-performance hardware accelerators. It features Intel Xeon 6342 processors providing a total of 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, running at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 10.67 GB of RAM per core.

The server integrates four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 320 GB of VRAM. This hardware configuration is supported by 900 GB of local SSD storage and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. Because this is a bare metal deployment, resources are fully dedicated without virtualization overhead, maximizing throughput for intensive workloads.

This server is designed for workloads requiring massive parallel processing capabilities and substantial memory capacity. Appropriate use cases include large-scale deep learning training, complex artificial intelligence inference, high-performance computing simulations, and data science pipelines. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare metal architecture and high GPU-to-CPU density provide a consolidated platform that reduces the need for multi-node clustering for large models.
description: Vultr vbm-48c-1024gb-4-a100-gpu is a dedicated bare metal server designed for high-performance computing and parallel processing workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6342 processor configuration delivering 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture at 2.8 GHz. The system features 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core, alongside 900 GB of local SSD storage. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 320 GB of VRAM, offering substantial computational power for demanding tasks. Operating without virtualization overhead, this bare metal instance delivers dedicated resource access. It is highly suited for deep learning training, large-scale AI inference, data science pipelines, and complex scientific simulations.
